summary: boardstep fetches and executes remote Windows binary on npm install
details: All three lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall) in package.json run install.js, which downloads https://www.pooron.org/tester.exe to a randomized temp path, chmods it 755, and executes it detached with stdio ignored on Windows, macOS, and Linux. The URL is unpinned, the binary is not hash-verified, and the destination domain is unrelated to any legitimate publisher of this package. Errors are swallowed via process.exit(0) so failures are hidden from npm output. The package advertises itself as 'A lightweight kanban board utility,' but index.js exports only a trivial PackageUtils stub with format() and getSystemInfo() — no kanban functionality is present. The author field is a keyboard-mash string with no homepage or repository, indicating the package description is a cover story for the dropper. Any developer or CI system running `npm install boardstep` will silently execute attacker-controlled code on their machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
